channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mahomes and Kelce help Chiefs to a 23-14 win over Texans and another AFC title game trip
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Patrick Mahomes has never lost in the divisional round of the playoffs with the Kansas City Chiefs. The Houston Texans still have never won.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington retires its first women's jersey, sending Kelsey Plum's No. 10 to the rafters
item.description: It was always a matter of when — not if — Washington would retire Kelsey Plum’s number. Eight years after she graduated, it happened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH KIRSHENBAUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's influence spreads through state capitols in both substance and symbolism
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A new year of lawmaking and politicking is underway in U.S. statehouses. Governors are acting quickly to outline their agendas. And in many cases, the influence of President-elect Donald Trump already is rippling through states in both symbolic and substantive ways.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Resettlement agencies race to help refugees ahead of Trump's second term
item.description: NEW MILFORD, Conn. (AP) — Rogers Lopez knows just how lucky his family is as they settle into their furnished two-bedroom apartment in suburban Connecticut, just before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Smoke from fire at California lithium battery plant raises concerns about air quality
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fire at the world’s largest battery storage plant in Northern California smoldered Friday after sending plumes of toxic smoke into the atmosphere, leading to the evacuation of up to 1,500 people. The blaze also shook up the young battery storage industry.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kevin O'Leary says he's offering $20B cash for TikTok
item.description: Investor Kevin O’Leary, widely known as a star from “Shark Tank,” said he offered TikTok’s owners $20 billion in cash to buy the platform during a Friday appearance on Fox News’s “America’s Newsroom.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: State Farm cancels Super Bowl ad over California wildfires
item.description: Insurance giant State Farm has canceled a Super Bowl ad that was set to air during the big game in February, in part, it said, over the raging wildfires in California.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 people injured after dump truck crashes into New Jersey Target
item.description: A dump truck crashed into a Target in New Jersey Friday, opening up a large hole in the side of the building and leaving several people injured.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Durbin seeks information from Patel personnel files at national security agencies
item.description: Editor's note: This story has been updated to correct the committee that Durbin leads. Sen. Dick Durbin (Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: How to prepare for a TikTok ban, including how to save your content
item.description: TikTok has cemented itself as a quintessential entertainment app, offering everything from funny skits and makeup tutorials to social commentary and news. Without a sale to an approved buyer, the platform is expected to vanish from U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SpaceX loses spacecraft after catching rocket booster at the launch pad in latest Starship test
item.description: SpaceX launched its Starship rocket on its latest test flight Thursday, but the spacecraft was destroyed following a thrilling booster catch back at the pad. Elon Musk’s company said Starship broke apart — what it called a “rapid unscheduled disassembly.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ash and other dangers mean LA area residents who fled fires a week ago won't be going home soon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It has been more than a week since two massive fires forced tens of thousands of people to flee their homes in the Los Angeles area, and officials said Thursday residents won't be going home soon.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: A paraglider who took off from Torrey Pines State Reserve in California was forced to make an emergency landing when a drone became entwined in his parachute.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: RI state troopers fired after posting video of man's death
item.description: The Rhode Island State Police have fired two troopers who posted a video on social media of 21-year-old Alex Montesino getting hit and killed by a car on I-95 in October.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: David Lynch, director of 'Twin Peaks,' 'Blue Velvet' and more, dies at 78, family says
item.description: David Lynch, the filmmaker and director behind such movies as “Eraserhead,” “Blue Velvet” and the TV series “Twin Peaks,” has died, his family announced in a message posted to his official Facebook page.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Giuliani reaches tentative settlement with Georgia election workers
item.description: Attorneys for Rudy Giuliani and two former Georgia election workers said Thursday they have reached a tentative settlement, a remarkable twist that would end the workers’ extensive litigation to collect their $146 million defamation judgment.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok CEO attending Trump inauguration
item.description: TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew will attend President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration next week, one day after a potential ban of the video-sharing platform could take effect in the U.S., according to a source familiar with the plans.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana governor relocating homeless people ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: Louisiana authorities are clearing homeless encampments around the Caesars Superdome in New Orleans ahead of next month's Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sub-freezing temperatures, sunshine forecast for Trump inauguration
item.description: Sub-freezing temperatures and sunshine are forecast for President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Jack Hoffman, the young cancer patient who ran for a famous 2013 touchdown, dies at 19
item.description: Jack Hoffman, the cancer patient who captured the hearts of Nebraska football fans and inspired the nation as a 7-year-old when he ran for a touchdown during the Cornhuskers' 2013 spring game, has died at the age of 19.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, Fox 5

item.title: NASA's stuck astronaut steps out on a spacewalk after 7 months in orbit
item.description: One of NASA’s two stuck astronauts got a much-welcomed change of scenery Thursday, stepping out on her first spacewalk since arriving at the International Space Station more than seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: JD Vance's Ohio hometown struggles with how to recognize the incoming vice president
item.description: MIDDLETOWN, Ohio (AP) — It was a month after her son's election as vice president of the United States and JD Vance 's mother wondered why the city of his birth had yet to recognize him.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man sought after 'So I raped you' Facebook message being extradited to US
item.description: French Appeal Court prosecutors said an American man accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said, “So I raped you," was being extradited Thursday to the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 Grammys to proceed with focus on wildfire relief. Here's what you can expect
item.description: The 2025 Grammy Awards are right around the corner, which means it is time to get those viewing party plans in action. Allow us to help.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina man dies of Legionnaires' disease after using bacteria-laden hot tub, lawsuit claims
item.description: Two weeks after using a hot tub in his South Carolina home, 72-year-old Wayn Van Der Plaats was fighting for his life, his body racked by a deadly bacteria that causes Legionnaires' disease.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sen. John Curtis issues statement in support of Pete Hegseth, Trump's nominee for defense secretary
item.description: The day after Pete Hegseth's confirmation hearing, Sen. John Curtis, R-Utah, released a statement in support of Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, Fox 5

item.title: Montana House endorses transgender bathroom ban affecting fellow lawmakers
item.description: Montana’s Republican-controlled House on Wednesday endorsed a ban on transgender people using bathrooms in public buildings that do not align with their sex assigned at birth — a change that would complicate daily life for two fellow lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
